---
title: "Basic Research and Bioinformatics Training"
categories: [Courses]
image: "image.jpg"
---
<div style="text-align: justify;">

In this course, foundational research skills and practical bioinformatics techniques, including scientific literature analysis, sequence exploration, and molecular visualization, will be learned by students.

**Course Overview:**

This training is designed for beginners and aspiring researchers who want to gain practical knowledge in basic research concepts and bioinformatics. Participants will learn how to approach scientific problems, analyze data, and apply computational tools to biological questions.

**What You Will Learn:**

- **Research Fundamentals:** Understanding research design, literature review, hypothesis formulation, and data interpretation  
- **Scientific Literature Analysis:** How to search, read, and summarize scientific articles efficiently  
- **Bioinformatics Tools:** Introduction to sequence retrieval, physicochemical analysis, subcellular localization, protein function prediction, and molecular visualization  
- **Practical Exercises:** Hands-on training using real datasets to reinforce learning  
- **Data Management & Reporting:** How to document findings and prepare reports for academic or research purposes

**Who Should Attend:**

- Students of life sciences, biotechnology, or related fields  
- Beginners in bioinformatics and computational biology  
- Anyone interested in learning research methodology and practical bioinformatics skills  

**Course Outcomes:**  
By the end of this training, participants will be able to:  

- Conduct literature searches and summarize findings effectively  
- Apply basic bioinformatics analyses on DNA, RNA, and protein sequences  
- Use molecular visualization tools to explore protein structures  
- Develop confidence in approaching small research projects

[Drop a message to the page](https://www.facebook.com/getlearnbioinformatics) if you are interested to do it.
</div>
